Done. I've also removed the "data" from the log, for fear of a security issue.
My main concern here is, without the null check, each time the Handler is instantiated, will it not create a new set of Kafkas?

maybe some thing like

Failed to convert data to json...
I think it would be ok to log the data tbh

Once a KafkaHandler is instantiated, a new SdcKafkaProucer and Consumer is instantiated in it, regardless if another KafkaHandler was created previously.

What we can do is create only one of the SdcKafkaProducer or COnsumer, depending on what's needed when that instance of KafkaHandler is created. That will cut down on logs and unneeded overheads.

Maybe even a check when the "sendNotification" etc is called, to see if sdcKafkaProducer is null, and to set it if it is.
